title: Federal effort to boost election worker protections fizzles
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — Federal proposals that would have significantly boosted security funding for election offices and heightened penalties for threatening their staff failed to advance this year, leaving state officials looking to their legislatures for support.

title: After Jan. 6: Congress born of chaos ends in achievement
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 117th Congress opened with the unfathomable Jan. 6, 2021, mob siege of the Capitol and is closing with unprecedented federal criminal referrals of the former president over the insurrection — all while conducting one of the most consequential legislative sessions in recent memory.

title: Georgia special grand jury wraps up probe of Trump, allies
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — A special grand jury investigating whether then-President Donald Trump and his allies illegally tried to overturn his defeat in the 2020 election in Georgia appears to be wrapping up its work, but many questions remain.

title: House passes $1.7 trillion spending bill with Ukraine aid
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — A $1.7 trillion spending bill financing federal agencies through September and providing more aid to a devastated Ukraine cleared the House on Friday as lawmakers raced to finish their work for the year and avoid a partial government shutdown.

title: Jan. 6 report blames Trump, aims to prevent return to power
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — A massive final report released by the House Jan. 6 committee late Thursday places the blame for the 2021 Capitol insurrection on one person: former President Donald Trump.

title: Congress approves new election rules in Jan. 6 response
shortDescription: Congress on Friday gave final passage to legislation changing the arcane law that governs the certification of a presidential contest, the strongest effort yet to avoid a repeat of Donald Trump's violence-inflaming push to reverse his loss in the 2020 election.

title: Cubans, Nicaraguans drive migration to US border in November
shortDescription: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Illegal border crossings by Cubans and Nicaraguans rose sharply in November while overall migration flows were little changed from October, U.S. authorities said Friday.The snapshot is the latest detailed account of who is crossing the border from Mexico amid preparations to end a Trump-era asylum ban.

title: New law ends COVID-19 vaccine mandate for US troops
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. military forces around the world will no longer be required to get the COVID-19 vaccine, after the mandate was lifted under an $858 billion defense spending bill passed by Congress and signed into law Friday by President Joe Biden.

title: Rabuka sworn in as Fiji prime minister after close election
shortDescription: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Sitiveni Rabuka was sworn in as Fiji’s prime minister on Saturday, capping a tense week in a fragile Pacific democracy where the former military commander first held office more than two decades ago.

title: China blasts US defense bill while Taiwan welcomes it
shortDescription: BEIJING (AP) — China blasted an annual U.S. defense spending bill for hyping up the “China threat" while Taiwan welcomed the legislation, saying it demonstrated U.S. support for the self-governing island that China says must come under its rule.

title: Bidens visit patients at Children’s National Hospital
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and his wife, first lady Jill Biden, visited Children's National Hospital on the Friday before Christmas to visit with patients and read a story.Sitting in front of a towering Christmas tree, the president helped hold the book while the first lady read “The Snowy Day,” an award-winning story about a boy named Peter and his adventures in the snow.

title: Spending bill secures funds for Native American health care
shortDescription: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Access to health care for Native Americans and Alaska Natives will be bolstered with funding included in a massive government spending bill awaiting President Joe Biden’s signature.

title: Jury selection for Proud Boys trial to resume next month
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jury selection for the seditious conspiracy trial of former Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio and four lieutenants will resume in early January after a holiday break, according to court records.

title: Jan. 6 report: Trump ‘lit that fire’ of Capitol insurrection
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Jan. 6 committee’s final report asserts that Donald Trump criminally engaged in a “multi-part conspiracy” to overturn the lawful results of the 2020 presidential election and failed to act to stop his supporters from attacking the Capitol, concluding an extraordinary 18-month investigation into the former president and the violent insurrection two years ago.

title: What Minnesota’s most diverse Legislature means for policy
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Minnesota convenes its most diverse Legislature to-date in January, with some of its newest members saying they plan to use their history-making elections to pass policies to protect the minority communities they represent.

title: Arizona lawyers: Kari Lake election loss lawsuit lacks merit
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Republican Kari Lake didn’t offer evidence to back her claims of widespread, intentional misconduct on Election Day at her two-day trial challenging her loss to Democrat Katie Hobbs in Arizona governor’s race, lawyers for the state said Thursday.

title: Abortion ban changes split Wisconsin GOP under veto threat
shortDescription: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin Republicans who control the Legislature are divided over whether to push for rape and incest abortion exceptions in the face of Democratic Gov. Tony Evers' pledge to veto any bill that keeps the state's 173-year-old ban in place.

title: Jan. 6 takeaways: From Trump’s lies to the ‘unimaginable’
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Jan. 6 committee released its final report Thursday on the “unimaginable” 2021 attack on the U.S. Capitol, a mob assault by supporters of the defeated president, Donald Trump, that shook the nation and exposed the fragility of American democracy.

title: White House: Russia’s Wagner received arms from North Korea
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House said Thursday that the Wagner Group, a private Russian military company, has taken delivery of an arms shipment from North Korea to help bolster its forces as it fights side-by-side with Russian troops in Ukraine.

title: Jan. 6 panel unveils report, describes Trump ‘conspiracy’
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Jan. 6 committee’s final report asserts that Donald Trump criminally engaged in a “multi-part conspiracy” to overturn the lawful results of the 2020 presidential election and failed to act to stop his supporters from attacking the Capitol, concluding an extraordinary 18-month investigation into the former president and the violent insurrection two years ago.

title: Pageantry evoking Churchill greets Zelenskyy in Washington
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — At a time of grave consequence, a wartime leader crossed the Atlantic to arrive at a White House decked in holiday decor to consult with the American president about a war in Europe.

title: Trump’s taxes: House OKs bill on IRS audits of presidents
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House took action Thursday after disclosures that the IRS never fully reviewed Donald Trump’s tax returns during his presidency, passing a bill that would require audits of any president’s income tax filings.

title: Jan. 6 witness recounts pressure campaign from Trump allies
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former White House aide Cassidy Hutchinson described to the House Jan.

title: Ally of ex-Pakistani PM Khan sacked as Punjab chief minister
shortDescription: ISLAMABAD, Pakistan (AP) — In a major political move, Pakistani authorities before dawn Friday removed Pervez Elahi as the top elected official in the country's populous eastern Punjab province, following his failure to win a vote of confidence from the provincial legislature.

title: NY legislators pass bill making them nation’s best-paid
shortDescription: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Just in time for Christmas, New York legislators returned to the state capital Thursday to give themselves a nice holiday gift: a pay raise that would make them the nation’s best-paid state lawmakers.

title: Senate passes $1.7 trillion bill to fund gov’t, aid Ukraine
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a massive $1.7 trillion spending bill Thursday that finances federal agencies through September and provides another significant round of military and economic aid to Ukraine one day after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's dramatic address to a joint meeting of Congress.

title: Jan. 6 takeaways: Power, pressure and a ‘moral struggle’
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Jan. 6 committee is releasing dozens of witness transcripts from its investigation into the 2021 Capitol attack, including Thursday's release of a previously unseen account from former White House aide Cassidy Hutchinson detailing a stunning campaign by Donald Trump's allies encouraging her to stay “loyal” as she testified.

title: With House shifting, Senate locks in Ukraine funds
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — On the heels of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s emotional appeal for continued U.S. support, the Senate voted Thursday to ensure the money keeps flowing for years, even after Republicans take control of the House.

title: EXPLAINER: What’s the debate over releasing Trump’s taxes?
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Democratic-controlled House Ways and Means Committee on Tuesday voted to release former President Donald Trump’s tax returns, raising the potential of additional revelations in the coming days related to the finances of the longtime businessman who broke political norms by refusing to voluntarily make public his returns as he sought the presidency.

title: El Paso city leaders call on Biden to visit the border amidst crisis
shortDescription: The mayor said emergency measure is "a band-aid on a broken system" and some council members say President Biden needs to see the crisis in El Paso.

title: Will Trump be charged in Georgia? A special grand jury will answer.
shortDescription: Will Donald Trump face charges in Georgia? A special grand jury is wrapping up its work investigating allegations of interference in the election.

title: This week: Congress avoids shutdown, Jan. 6 committee releases final report
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee ended their work this week with a final meeting and over 800-page report. Here's what else happened this week in politics.

title: Jan. 6 report: Trump floated plan for 10,000 troops to protect him - recap of findings
shortDescription: The House committee investigating the Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S. Capitol released its highly-anticipated final report. Read it here.

title: Ukraine aid, pay raises, TikTok ban: Congress passes $1.7T spending bill
shortDescription: The package includes changes to retirement benefits, new protections for pregnant workers and Ukraine aid. A larger child tax credit was not included.

title: Congress revises Electoral Count Act that Trump used to pressure Pence on Jan. 6
shortDescription: Electoral Count Act reform, one of the top recommendations of the Jan. 6 committee, has passed Congress through the spending bill.

title: Senate sends $1.7 trillion spending bill to House: What made the cut
shortDescription: The bipartisan bill provides billions for Ukraine, pay raises for troops, and disaster aid. Extension of a pandemic-era child tax credit was left out.

title: Ex-Trump aide: 'Trump world' tried to stifle testimony – takeaways from Jan. 6 records
shortDescription: Cassidy Hutchinson's transcript released Thursday illustrated how the star Jan. 6 committee witness wrangled behind-the-scenes to provide testimony.

title: Jan. 6 recap: Watergate book inspired ex-Trump aide to testify
shortDescription: The House committee investigating the Capitol attack on Jan. 6, 2021, is expected to release its final report Thursday.

title: OnPolitics: Here's what's in Congress' $1.7 trillion spending package
shortDescription: The Senate overwhelmingly passed a $1.7 trillion spending package today. Here's what made the cut.

title: 'Alive and kicking': Ukraine president Zelenskyy presses Congress for more aid
shortDescription: Biden promised staunch support for Ukraine as he welcomed Volodymyr Zelenskyy to the White House during the Ukraine leader's whirlwind trip to the US.

title: Zelenskyy and Biden meet at White House
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy visited the U.S. in his first foreign trip since war began in his country.

title: Joe Biden welcomes Zelenskyy at White House
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's visit to the U.S. comes as Joe Biden and Congress reaffirm their commitment to Ukraine.

title: Notable dignitaries who have addressed Congress
shortDescription: Visiting foreign leaders address Joint Meetings of Congress often during state visits to the United States.

title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy to meet with Biden, address Congress
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will make his first trip to Washington since Russia began its violent invasion of Ukraine in February.

title: Supreme Court denies Trump effort to keep tax returns from House panel
shortDescription: Trump's record at the Supreme Court is not particularly good, even though he nominated three of the court's current associate justices.

title: House panel votes to release Trump tax information
shortDescription: The House Ways and Means Committee got copies of Donald Trump's tax returns from the Treasury Department a few weeks ago after a long legal battle.

title: House panel votes on Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: The Democrat-led House Ways and Means Committee voted to release some of former President Donald Trump's tax returns in the next few days.

title: Will Hope Hicks' complete Jan. 6 testimony be damning for Trump?
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee revealed damaging testimony from Trump loyalist Hope Hicks on Monday. With full report and transcripts coming, does it have more?

title: Biden urges Supreme Court to let it end Title 42 migrant program
shortDescription: Biden's response came a day after Chief Justice Roberts temporarily blocked the Biden administration from ending Title 42 migrant expulsions.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.

title: It Took Just Five Months to Lose Her Rights to Her Kids Forever
shortDescription: Twenty-five years ago, Congress passed a law aimed at speeding up adoptions of children languishing in foster care. In the process, it destroyed hundreds of thousands of families through the termination of parental rights.

title: Congress and Industry Leaders Call for Crackdown on Hospice Fraud
shortDescription: Following a ProPublica-New Yorker investigation into the hospice industry, members of the Comprehensive Care Caucus and national trade groups are demanding reform.

title: Congress Votes to Expand U.S. Power to Prosecute International War Crimes
shortDescription: Passed after an address by Ukraine’s leader, the bill would allow foreigners in the United States who are suspected of war crimes to be tried even if the crime was overseas and the victim was not American.By Aishvarya Kavi

title: Adam Schiff: My Fellow Members of the Jan. 6 Committee and I Don’t Want You to Forget About ‘the Republican Congressmen’
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee found that multiple laws were violated in an attempt to overturn the 2020 election, and not just by Donald Trump’s foot soldiers.By Adam B. Schiff

title: How a Bipartisan Senate Group Addressed a Flaw Exposed by Jan. 6
shortDescription: Democrats and Republicans managed to come together to update the archaic Electoral Count Act after they recognized it could again be abused to subvert the presidential vote.By Carl Hulse

title: Senate Passes $1.7 Trillion Spending Bill, in Bid to Avert Government Shutdown
shortDescription: The bill, which would provide nearly $50 billion in aid to Ukraine, now heads to the House for a vote where it is expected to pass before being sent to President Biden.By Emily Cochrane
